articles.headline: Government Unveils 'Viksit Bharat 2047' Advanced Manufacturing & Skill Development Policy
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved a comprehensive policy framework aimed at boosting advanced manufacturing capabilities and skill development across key sectors.
• The policy integrates with the 'PM Gati Shakti' master plan, ensuring seamless infrastructure support for new industrial corridors and clusters.
• Focus areas include robotics, AI-driven manufacturing, green technologies, and high-precision engineering, with significant incentives for R&D.
• A new National Skill Development Mission 2.0 is launched to re-skill and up-skill the workforce for future industries, linking education with industry demands.
• The initiative seeks to position India as a global manufacturing hub and achieve the 'Viksit Bharat' vision by 2047.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Development processes and the development industry
Skill development
mainsPoints: Significance of manufacturing for economic growth and employment generation.
Role of skill development in demographic dividend utilization.
Challenges and opportunities in transitioning to advanced manufacturing.
Impact of integrated infrastructure planning (PM Gati Shakti) on industrial development.
Government's long-term vision for India's economic future.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of modern factories and skilled workers, with 'Viksit Bharat 2047' logo.
articles.trivia: The concept of 'Viksit Bharat' or Developed India by 2047 aligns with India's 100th year of independence.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Directs Government to Strengthen Data Privacy Framework
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark ruling, emphasized the urgent need for a robust data protection law and privacy framework in India.
• The court highlighted gaps in existing regulations, particularly concerning personal data collection by private entities and state surveillance.
• It directed the Union Government to expedite the legislative process for a comprehensive data protection bill, ensuring fundamental rights are upheld.
• The ruling stemmed from petitions challenging certain aspects of digital data collection and sharing practices.
• Experts believe this judgment will significantly influence the upcoming parliamentary session and the final shape of India's data privacy legislation.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions
Fundamental Rights
Information Technology
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court's stance on data privacy
Need for robust data protection law
mainsPoints: Discuss the implications of the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right in the digital age.
Analyze the challenges in balancing state security concerns with individual data privacy.
Evaluate the potential impact of a comprehensive data protection law on India's digital economy.
Critically examine the role of judiciary in safeguarding fundamental rights in the absence of specific legislation.
articles.imageDescription: Gavel and digital data flowing, symbolizing justice and technology.
articles.trivia: The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared the Right to Privacy a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate, Focuses on Liquidity Management Amidst Global Volatility
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) concluded its latest Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) meeting, deciding to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
• The decision comes amidst persistent global economic uncertainties, geopolitical tensions, and moderate domestic inflation pressures.
• RBI Governor highlighted the central bank's commitment to maintaining financial stability and supporting growth while managing inflation expectations.
• The MPC reiterated its withdrawal of accommodation stance, signaling a cautious approach to future policy adjustments.
• Liquidity management measures are expected to be fine-tuned to ensure adequate credit flow to productive sectors of the economy.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning
mobilization of resources
growth
development and employment
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: Factors influencing MPC decision (global volatility, domestic inflation)
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by RBI in balancing inflation control and economic growth.
Discuss the effectiveness of monetary policy tools in addressing supply-side inflation.
Examine the impact of global economic developments on India's monetary policy decisions.
Evaluate the role of liquidity management in ensuring financial stability and credit availability.
articles.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building with currency symbols.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in 2016 to bring transparency and accountability in deciding monetary policy.

articles.headline: India-ASEAN Summit Concludes with Focus on Enhanced Connectivity and Digital Partnership
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-ASEAN Summit concluded with leaders reaffirming commitment to strengthening the Comprehensive Strategic Partnership.
• Key outcomes included agreements on boosting physical and digital connectivity, focusing on projects under the Act East Policy.
• Discussions emphasized cooperation in emerging technologies, cybersecurity, and digital public infrastructure development.
• Both sides stressed the importance of a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific region, adhering to international law.
• India reiterated its support for ASEAN centrality and its role in regional peace and prosperity.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India's interests
prelimsPoints: Key focus areas of India-ASEAN cooperation (connectivity, digital, Indo-Pacific)
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of ASEAN for India's Act East Policy and its broader Indo-Pacific strategy.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in enhancing connectivity between India and Southeast Asian nations.
Evaluate India's role in promoting a free and open Indo-Pacific region in collaboration with ASEAN.
Examine the potential of digital partnership in strengthening India-ASEAN ties.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and ASEAN member states.
articles.trivia: India became a Sectoral Partner of ASEAN in 1992, a Dialogue Partner in 1995, and a Summit-level Partner in 2002.

articles.headline: Teachers' Day 2025: PM Reviews NEP Implementation, Lauds Educators' Role
articles.explainer:
• On the occasion of Teachers' Day, Prime Minister Narendra Modi addressed educators nationwide, acknowledging their pivotal role in nation-building.
• The PM reviewed the progress of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 implementation, particularly focusing on teacher training and digital education initiatives.
• New programs for continuous professional development for teachers were announced, emphasizing pedagogical innovations and technology integration.
• The government reiterated its commitment to empowering teachers and ensuring quality education access for all, in line with NEP objectives.
• Celebrations across the country highlighted inspiring stories of teachers making significant contributions to society.
gsSubTopics: Social empowerment
Development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
prelimsPoints: Significance of Teachers' Day (birthday of Dr. Sarvepalli Radhakrishnan)
Key aspects of NEP 2020 related to teachers
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical role of teachers in implementing the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
Analyze the challenges faced by the Indian education system in attracting and retaining quality teachers.
Suggest measures to enhance the professional development and motivation of educators in India.
Evaluate the impact of digital integration on teaching methodologies and learning outcomes.
articles.imageDescription: Prime Minister interacting with teachers, symbolizing appreciation and policy review.
articles.trivia: Teachers' Day in India is celebrated on 5th September, marking the birth anniversary of Dr. Sarvepalli Radhakrishnan, India's second President and a renowned philosopher.

articles.headline: ISRO Intensifies Gaganyaan Preparations: Focus on Crew Module & Abort Tests
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced accelerated preparations for the Gaganyaan mission, India's first human spaceflight program.
• Key focus is on rigorous testing of the crew module's life support systems and re-entry capabilities.
• A series of uncrewed test flights, including critical abort missions to ensure crew safety, are scheduled in the coming months.
• Astronaut training is progressing well, with specialized modules focusing on mission specifics and emergency procedures.
• Gaganyaan aims to demonstrate India's capability to launch humans into low Earth orbit and bring them back safely.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology
indigenization of technology and developing new technology
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of human spaceflight missions like Gaganyaan for India's scientific and technological advancement.
Analyze the challenges involved in developing indigenous capabilities for human spaceflight.
Evaluate the potential spin-off benefits of space technology for various sectors in India.
Examine the role of international collaboration in India's space program.
articles.imageDescription: Artist's impression of the Gaganyaan crew module in orbit.
articles.trivia: The Gaganyaan mission aims to send a 3-member crew to an orbit of 400 km for a 3-day mission and bring them back safely.

articles.headline: India Launches National Green Hydrogen Mission Phase II, Targets Export Hub Status
articles.explainer:
• Building on the success of Phase I, the government launched Phase II of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, with enhanced targets for production and export.
• The new phase includes greater incentives for green hydrogen production, storage, and infrastructure development, attracting both domestic and international investments.
• Focus areas include developing green hydrogen corridors, promoting its use in hard-to-abate sectors like steel and fertilizers, and establishing R&D centers.
• The policy aims to reduce India's reliance on fossil fuel imports and position the country as a leading global exporter of green hydrogen and its derivatives.
• Environmental and economic benefits, including job creation, are central to the mission's long-term objectives.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation
environmental impact assessment
Infrastructure: Energy
prelimsPoints: Objectives of National Green Hydrogen Mission Phase II
Target sectors for green hydrogen use
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and climate change commitments.
Analyze the economic and technological challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production in India.
Evaluate the potential of green hydrogen in decarbonizing hard-to-abate industrial sectors.
Examine how government policies and incentives can foster a robust green hydrogen ecosystem.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen production facility with wind turbines and solar panels.
articles.trivia: Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water into hydrogen and oxygen using renewable electricity, making it a zero-emission fuel.

articles.headline: Government Strengthens Cybersecurity Framework for Critical National Infrastructure
articles.explainer:
• In response to increasing cyber threats, the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) announced a strengthened cybersecurity framework.
• The new guidelines mandate enhanced security protocols and regular audits for all critical national infrastructure sectors, including energy, finance, and telecommunications.
• A dedicated Cyber Incident Response Team (CIRT) for critical infrastructure is being established, with closer coordination among various agencies.
• The policy emphasizes public-private partnerships for threat intelligence sharing and capacity building.
• The move aims to protect vital services from sophisticated cyberattacks and ensure national security.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks
role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges
basics of cyber security
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving nature of cyber threats and their implications for national security and economic stability.
Analyze the challenges in securing critical national infrastructure from sophisticated cyberattacks.
Examine the role of government, private sector, and international cooperation in building a resilient cybersecurity ecosystem.
Suggest policy measures to enhance India's cyber defense capabilities and capacity building.
articles.imageDescription: Digital lock icon over a cityscape, representing cybersecurity for infrastructure.
articles.trivia: India's National Cyber Security Policy was launched in 2013 to protect information infrastructure and reduce cybercrime.

articles.headline: GST Council Meets, Discusses Rate Rationalization and Compliance Simplification
articles.explainer:
• The GST Council convened its quarterly meeting to review tax collection trends and discuss potential reforms to the Goods and Services Tax regime.
• A key agenda item was the rationalization of GST rates for certain goods and services, aimed at simplifying the tax structure and boosting consumption.
• Discussions also focused on enhancing compliance through technological improvements and addressing issues faced by small and medium enterprises (SMEs).
• States presented their revenue concerns and sought clarity on compensation mechanisms for any revenue shortfalls.
• The Council is expected to announce specific recommendations aimed at making the GST system more efficient and equitable.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning
mobilization of resources
growth
development and employment
Government Budgeting
prelimsPoints: Issues faced by states regarding GST
mainsPoints: Analyze the impact of GST on India's economy and fiscal federalism since its implementation.
Discuss the challenges and benefits of GST rate rationalization for businesses and consumers.
Examine the role of technology in improving GST compliance and preventing tax evasion.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the GST Council as a unique institution for cooperative federalism in India.
articles.imageDescription: GST logo with Indian currency notes.
articles.trivia: The GST Council is a joint forum of the Centre and States, chaired by the Union Finance Minister, making it a unique example of cooperative federalism.

articles.headline: New Urban Mobility Plan to Focus on Tier-2 and Tier-3 Cities
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs announced a new comprehensive Urban Mobility Plan targeting sustainable transportation solutions for Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities.
• The plan emphasizes development of integrated public transport networks, non-motorized transport infrastructure, and smart traffic management systems.
• Funding mechanisms will include central government grants, state contributions, and private sector investments, promoting green mobility solutions.
• The initiative aims to reduce traffic congestion, improve air quality, and enhance the quality of life in rapidly urbanizing smaller cities.
• Capacity building for urban local bodies and adoption of best practices from larger cities are also key components.
gsSubTopics: Urbanization, their problems and their remedies
Infrastructure: Urban
prelimsPoints: Ministry responsible for Urban Mobility Plan
Focus of the new plan (Tier 2/3 cities)
Key features (public transport, NMT, smart traffic)
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges of urban mobility in rapidly growing Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities in India.
Analyze the importance of an integrated approach to urban transportation for sustainable development.
Evaluate the role of government policies and private investment in promoting green mobility solutions.
Suggest innovative strategies to improve public transport and non-motorized transport infrastructure in smaller cities.
articles.imageDescription: Modern public transport in a bustling city, representing urban development.
articles.trivia: India is projected to have 50% of its population living in urban areas by 2047, making urban planning crucial.

articles.headline: PM-KISAN: Nationwide Verification Drive to Ensure Eligibility and Prevent Fraud
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare initiated a nationwide verification drive for beneficiaries under the Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN) scheme.
• The drive aims to identify and remove ineligible beneficiaries while ensuring that financial assistance reaches genuine farmers.
• Leveraging Aadhaar and land record integration, the verification process seeks to enhance transparency and reduce fraudulent claims.
• Farmers are urged to update their KYC details and land records to avoid disruption in benefits.
• The government reaffirms its commitment to supporting small and marginal farmers through direct income support.
gsSubTopics: Major crops-cropping patterns in various parts of the country
different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage
transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints
e-technology in the aid of farmers
prelimsPoints: Role of Aadhaar and land records
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of direct income support schemes like PM-KISAN for enhancing farmers' income and welfare.
Analyze the challenges associated with beneficiary identification and prevention of fraud in large-scale welfare schemes.
Evaluate the role of technology (Aadhaar, land records) in improving the efficiency and transparency of DBT programs.
Suggest measures to strengthen the implementation and monitoring of agricultural support schemes.
articles.imageDescription: Farmer in a field, with a digital overlay showing DBT in action.
articles.trivia: PM-KISAN scheme provides an income support of Rs. 6,000 per year to all eligible farmer families across the country.

articles.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Expands Services, Focus on Telemedicine Integration
articles.explainer:
• The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) announced the expansion of its services, aiming for wider adoption of digital health records and telemedicine.
• New features include enhanced interoperability between healthcare providers and integration with more private health applications.
• A major push is being made for telemedicine services, especially in rural and remote areas, to improve access to specialist consultations.
• The mission seeks to create a seamless online platform for patients to access their health information and medical services.
• Training programs for healthcare professionals on digital tools and data security are also being scaled up.
gsSubTopics: Development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
e-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential
prelimsPoints: Benefits for patients and healthcare providers
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of digital health initiatives like ABDM in improving healthcare access and efficiency in India.
Analyze the challenges in implementing a nationwide digital health ecosystem, particularly in terms of data privacy and infrastructure.
Evaluate the role of telemedicine in bridging the healthcare gap in rural and remote areas.
Suggest measures to ensure equitable access to digital health services for all sections of society.
articles.imageDescription: Digital interface showing health records and telemedicine consultation.
articles.trivia: The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission aims to develop the backbone necessary to support the integrated digital health infrastructure of the country.

articles.headline: India Continues G20 Follow-up, Champions Global South Priorities
articles.explainer:
• A year after its successful G20 presidency, India is actively engaged in follow-up initiatives, pushing for the implementation of outcomes from the New Delhi Declaration.
• India continues to champion the priorities of the Global South, advocating for equitable representation in global governance and addressing development challenges.
• Focus areas include climate finance, sustainable development goals (SDGs), and reforms of multilateral development banks.
• Bilateral and multilateral engagements are underway to build consensus on key global issues, including food and energy security.
• India's diplomatic efforts are seen as crucial in maintaining momentum for a more inclusive and representative global order.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India's interests
prelimsPoints: Key outcomes of India's G20 Presidency
mainsPoints: Analyze India's increasing role in shaping the global agenda through forums like G20, particularly as a voice for the Global South.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in reforming multilateral development banks to address contemporary global financial needs.
Evaluate India's efforts in mobilizing global action on climate finance and achieving Sustainable Development Goals.
Examine the significance of the New Delhi Declaration and its implications for future global cooperation.
articles.imageDescription: G20 logo with flags of member countries, emphasizing India's leadership.
articles.trivia: India's G20 theme 'Vasudhaiva Kutumbakam' or 'One Earth · One Family · One Future' resonated globally.

articles.headline: ASI Initiates Major Restoration Project for Ancient Heritage Sites
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) launched a large-scale restoration and conservation project for several ancient heritage sites across the country.
• The initiative focuses on preserving architectural integrity, using traditional building materials and techniques, and integrating modern conservation science.
• Sites include lesser-known temples, ancient stepwells, and historical monuments, aiming to boost cultural tourism and local economies.
• Community engagement and skill development for local artisans in heritage conservation are integral parts of the project.
• The project also involves digital documentation and 3D mapping of the sites for future reference and virtual accessibility.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of preserving India's rich cultural heritage for national identity and future generations.
Analyze the challenges faced by organizations like ASI in conserving ancient monuments amidst urbanization and environmental degradation.
Evaluate the potential of cultural tourism as a driver of economic growth and local development.
Suggest innovative approaches for community involvement and technological integration in heritage conservation efforts.
articles.imageDescription: Workers carefully restoring an ancient temple facade.
articles.trivia: The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) was founded in 1861 by Alexander Cunningham, who became its first Director-General.

articles.headline: Defence Ministry Boosts Indigenous Manufacturing with New Procurement Policy
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence introduced a revised Defence Acquisition Procedure (DAP) aimed at further promoting indigenous manufacturing and reducing import dependence.
• The new policy prioritizes procurement from Indian vendors, with specific provisions for start-ups and MSMEs in the defence sector.
• Emphasis is placed on 'Design and Development' within India, fostering innovation and self-reliance in critical defence technologies.
• Long-term agreements for technology transfer and co-production with foreign partners are encouraged, but with mandatory indigenous content requirements.
• The move aligns with the 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiative, seeking to transform India into a defence manufacturing hub.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Organized crime and terrorism
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
indigenization of technology and developing new technology
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic necessity of indigenous defence manufacturing for India's national security and economic sovereignty.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in achieving self-reliance in critical defence technologies.
Evaluate the impact of government policies like 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' and revised DAP on the growth of India's defence industrial base.
Suggest measures to foster innovation and private sector participation in the defence manufacturing ecosystem.
articles.imageDescription: Modern defence equipment manufactured in India.
articles.trivia: India is one of the largest importers of defence equipment globally, but aims to become an exporter through indigenization efforts.

articles.headline: ICAR Promotes Climate-Resilient Agriculture Practices Across States
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR) intensified its efforts to promote climate-resilient agriculture (CRA) practices among farmers nationwide.
• Focus areas include drought-resistant crop varieties, efficient water management techniques, and agroforestry systems.
• ICAR is collaborating with state agricultural universities and Krishi Vigyan Kendras (KVKs) to disseminate knowledge and provide training to farmers.
• The initiative aims to enhance farmers' adaptive capacity to climate change impacts and ensure food security in vulnerable regions.
• Policy support for adoption of CRA practices, including subsidies and market linkages, is also being advocated.
gsSubTopics: Major crops-cropping patterns in various parts of the country
different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage
transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints
e-technology in the aid of farmers
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation
environmental impact assessment
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing vulnerability of Indian agriculture to climate change and its implications for food security.
Analyze the various strategies and practices involved in promoting climate-resilient agriculture.
Evaluate the role of research institutions and extension services (like KVKs) in disseminating agricultural innovations to farmers.
Suggest policy interventions to encourage widespread adoption of sustainable and climate-resilient farming practices.
articles.imageDescription: Farmers inspecting drought-resistant crops in a field.
articles.trivia: ICAR is an autonomous organization under the Department of Agricultural Research and Education (DARE), Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare, Government of India.

articles.headline: India Semiconductor Mission Reports Significant Progress, Attracts New Fab Investments
articles.explainer:
• The India Semiconductor Mission (ISM) announced significant progress in its efforts to establish a robust semiconductor manufacturing ecosystem in the country.
• Several new proposals for semiconductor fabrication (fab) units and ATMP (Assembly, Testing, Marking, and Packaging) facilities have been approved, attracting both domestic and foreign investors.
• The mission is focusing on developing a skilled workforce for the semiconductor industry through specialized training programs and academic collaborations.
• Incentives under the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme are proving effective in stimulating investment in this high-tech sector.
• India aims to become a key player in the global semiconductor supply chain, reducing reliance on imports and enhancing strategic autonomy.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Investment models
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of building an indigenous semiconductor manufacturing ecosystem for India's economic and technological sovereignty.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in attracting significant investments and developing a skilled workforce for the semiconductor industry.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government policies, such as the PLI scheme and ISM, in fostering growth in high-tech manufacturing.
Examine the geopolitical implications of global semiconductor supply chain vulnerabilities and India's role in addressing them.
articles.imageDescription: Microchip on a circuit board, symbolizing semiconductor manufacturing.
articles.trivia: The global semiconductor industry is highly concentrated, with Taiwan and South Korea dominating advanced chip manufacturing.

articles.headline: Animal Welfare Board Proposes Stricter Rules for Pet Shops and Breeders
articles.explainer:
• The Animal Welfare Board of India (AWBI) submitted a proposal to the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change for stricter regulations on pet shops and animal breeders.
• The proposed rules aim to curb illegal breeding, prevent cruelty, and ensure humane treatment of animals sold as pets.
• Key provisions include mandatory registration for all breeders and pet shops, strict health and hygiene standards, and limits on animal stocking.
• Public consultations were held, with strong support from animal rights organizations for enhanced protection of companion animals.
• The new regulations are expected to bring much-needed accountability and transparency to the pet trade industry.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation
environmental impact assessment
keyTerms: Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act
prelimsPoints: Aim of the rules (curb illegal breeding, humane treatment)
mainsPoints: Discuss the ethical and legal aspects of animal welfare in India, particularly concerning companion animals.
Analyze the challenges in regulating the unorganized pet trade and breeding industry.
Evaluate the effectiveness of existing laws and propose measures for stronger enforcement of animal protection norms.
Examine the societal implications of animal cruelty and the importance of responsible pet ownership.
articles.imageDescription: Healthy puppies in a clean environment, symbolizing good animal welfare.
articles.trivia: The Animal Welfare Board of India is a statutory advisory body established under Section 4 of the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act, 1960.

articles.headline: Indian Railways Announces New Dedicated Freight Corridor for North-East Region
articles.explainer:
• Indian Railways unveiled plans for a new dedicated freight corridor aimed at enhancing connectivity and economic development in the North-East region.
• The corridor will facilitate faster movement of goods, reduce logistics costs, and provide better access to markets for agricultural and industrial products.
• The project is part of the broader 'Gati Shakti' initiative, integrating with multi-modal transport infrastructure.
• Environmental impact assessments and land acquisition processes are underway, with a commitment to sustainable development.
• This strategic infrastructure push is expected to boost trade with neighboring countries and unlock the region's economic potential.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Development processes and the development industry
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of improving railway infrastructure in India's North-East region for national integration and economic development.
Analyze how dedicated freight corridors can enhance logistics efficiency and reduce transportation costs for industries and agriculture.
Evaluate the role of multi-modal connectivity projects like PM Gati Shakti in transforming India's infrastructure landscape.
Examine the potential impact of improved connectivity on the 'Act East Policy' and India's trade with Southeast Asian countries.
articles.imageDescription: A modern freight train speeding through a scenic landscape in North East India.
articles.trivia: The North-East Frontier Railway (NFR) is one of the 17 railway zones in India and covers the entire North-Eastern region.

highlights: Government launches 'Viksit Bharat 2047' policy to boost advanced manufacturing and skill development, integrating with PM Gati Shakti.
Supreme Court directs government to strengthen data privacy framework, emphasizing the need for robust legislation.
RBI maintains repo rate at 6.5%, focusing on liquidity management amidst global uncertainties and domestic inflation.
India-ASEAN Summit concludes with focus on enhanced connectivity, digital partnership, and a free Indo-Pacific.
ISRO intensifies Gaganyaan preparations, conducting rigorous tests of crew module and abort systems for human spaceflight mission.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the 'National Green Hydrogen Mission' recently in news, consider the following statements:
1. It aims to make India a net exporter of green hydrogen by 2030.
2. Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water using electricity from renewable energy sources.
3. The mission focuses on decarbonizing hard-to-abate sectors like steel and fertilizers.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1: The article mentions the mission aims to 'position the country as a leading global exporter of green hydrogen and its derivatives' which aligns with the overall goal for 2030. Statement 2: Green hydrogen is indeed produced via electrolysis using renewable electricity. Statement 3: The article explicitly states 'promoting its use in hard-to-abate sectors like steel and fertilizers'. All three statements are correct.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is the primary objective of the 'Viksit Bharat 2047' Advanced Manufacturing & Skill Development Policy, as mentioned in the news?
options: (a) To reduce India's import dependence on crude oil.
(b) To establish India as a global manufacturing hub and achieve developed nation status by 2047.
(c) To focus solely on agricultural productivity and farmer welfare.
(d) To promote traditional handicrafts and rural industries.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) To establish India as a global manufacturing hub and achieve developed nation status by 2047.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The lead article states, 'The initiative seeks to position India as a global manufacturing hub and achieve the 'Viksit Bharat' vision by 2047.' While other options might be related to other government initiatives, this is the primary objective of the mentioned policy.

prelimsMocks.question:
Regarding the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM), which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. It aims to create an integrated digital health infrastructure for India.
2. It focuses on facilitating telemedicine services, especially in rural areas.
3. It allows patients to access their health information and medical services online.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: The article explicitly states that ABDM aims to 'create a seamless online platform for patients to access their health information and medical services' and that 'A major push is being made for telemedicine services, especially in rural and remote areas'. It also mentions 'the expansion of its services, aiming for wider adoption of digital health records', which implies an integrated digital health infrastructure. All statements are correct.

prelimsMocks.question: Teachers' Day in India is celebrated on 5th September to commemorate the birth anniversary of:
prelimsMocks.explanation: The trivia section of the Teachers' Day article states, 'Teachers' Day in India is celebrated on 5th September, marking the birth anniversary of Dr. Sarvepalli Radhakrishnan, India's second President and a renowned philosopher.'

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding India's Defence Acquisition Procedure (DAP):
1. It primarily aims to increase India's reliance on imported defence equipment.
2. The revised DAP prioritizes procurement from Indian vendors, including start-ups and MSMEs.
3. It encourages 'Design and Development' within India for critical defence technologies.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect; the DAP aims to 'reduce import dependence' and promote indigenous manufacturing. Statement 2 is correct, as the article mentions 'prioritizes procurement from Indian vendors, with specific provisions for start-ups and MSMEs'. Statement 3 is also correct, as 'Emphasis is placed on 'Design and Development' within India'.

mainsMocks.question: The recent Supreme Court directive on strengthening India's data privacy framework highlights the evolving challenges in safeguarding fundamental rights in the digital age. Discuss the implications of this directive for both the state and private entities, and suggest key features of a robust data protection law for India. (15 Marks)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors; Fundamental Rights.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the Supreme Court's stance on data privacy and its connection to fundamental rights. Discuss implications for the state (surveillance, data handling) and private entities (data collection, processing, consent). Outline essential features of a robust data protection law (e.g., consent, purpose limitation, data minimization, right to be forgotten, independent regulatory authority, cross-border data flow norms, penalties).

mainsMocks.question: India's push for advanced manufacturing and skill development, as envisioned in the 'Viksit Bharat 2047' policy, is critical for leveraging its demographic dividend. Analyze the challenges and opportunities in transforming India into a global manufacturing hub, and evaluate how the integration with 'PM Gati Shakti' can accelerate this vision. (15 Marks)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment; Infrastructure; Skill development.
mainsMocks.approach: Define the 'Viksit Bharat 2047' vision and the policy's aims. Discuss challenges for manufacturing (infrastructure gaps, technology adoption, skilled labor shortage, ease of doing business). Highlight opportunities (large domestic market, young workforce, government incentives, geopolitical shifts). Explain how PM Gati Shakti addresses infrastructure bottlenecks, multi-modal connectivity, and integrated planning, thereby supporting manufacturing growth.

mainsMocks.question: The National Green Hydrogen Mission, now in its second phase, is a cornerstone of India's strategy for energy security and climate change mitigation. Examine the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India, and discuss the technological and economic hurdles that need to be overcome to achieve its ambitious targets. (10 Marks)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation; Infrastructure: Energy; Science and Technology.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the National Green Hydrogen Mission and its objectives. Explain the strategic importance (energy independence, decarbonization of industries, export potential, climate commitments). Discuss technological hurdles (electrolyser efficiency, storage, transport, R&D) and economic hurdles (high production cost, infrastructure investment, market creation, policy support, financing). Conclude with the potential benefits.

quickBytes.text: The 'Viksit Bharat 2047' vision aims for India to become a developed nation by its 100th year of independence.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) of RBI consists of six members, three from RBI and three appointed by the government.
ASEAN was established in 1967 in Bangkok, Thailand, with the signing of the ASEAN Declaration (Bangkok Declaration).
The Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act was enacted in 1960 to prevent the infliction of unnecessary pain or suffering on animals.
ISRO's Mars Orbiter Mission (Mangalyaan) was India's first interplanetary mission, launched in 2013.
GST Council is chaired by the Union Finance Minister and includes State Finance Ministers as members.
The National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 aims for universal access to quality education from pre-school to higher education.
Green hydrogen is considered a key fuel for the future as its production involves zero greenhouse gas emissions.
The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) is responsible for archaeological research and the protection of cultural heritage.
The 'Atmanirbhar Bharat Abhiyan' aims to make India self-reliant across various sectors, including defence.

GS1.headline: Teachers' Day 2025: PM Reviews NEP Implementation, Lauds Educators' Role
GS1.explainer:
• On the occasion of Teachers' Day, Prime Minister Narendra Modi addressed educators nationwide, acknowledging their pivotal role in nation-building.
• The PM reviewed the progress of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 implementation, particularly focusing on teacher training and digital education initiatives.
• New programs for continuous professional development for teachers were announced, emphasizing pedagogical innovations and technology integration.
• The government reiterated its commitment to empowering teachers and ensuring quality education access for all, in line with NEP objectives.
• Celebrations across the country highlighted inspiring stories of teachers making significant contributions to society.
gsSubTopics: Social empowerment
Development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
prelimsPoints: Significance of Teachers' Day (birthday of Dr. Sarvepalli Radhakrishnan)
Key aspects of NEP 2020 related to teachers
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical role of teachers in implementing the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
Analyze the challenges faced by the Indian education system in attracting and retaining quality teachers.
Suggest measures to enhance the professional development and motivation of educators in India.
Evaluate the impact of digital integration on teaching methodologies and learning outcomes.
GS1.imageDescription: Prime Minister interacting with teachers, symbolizing appreciation and policy review.
GS1.trivia: Teachers' Day in India is celebrated on 5th September, marking the birth anniversary of Dr. Sarvepalli Radhakrishnan, India's second President and a renowned philosopher.

GS1.headline: New Urban Mobility Plan to Focus on Tier-2 and Tier-3 Cities
GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs announced a new comprehensive Urban Mobility Plan targeting sustainable transportation solutions for Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities.
• The plan emphasizes development of integrated public transport networks, non-motorized transport infrastructure, and smart traffic management systems.
• Funding mechanisms will include central government grants, state contributions, and private sector investments, promoting green mobility solutions.
• The initiative aims to reduce traffic congestion, improve air quality, and enhance the quality of life in rapidly urbanizing smaller cities.
• Capacity building for urban local bodies and adoption of best practices from larger cities are also key components.
gsSubTopics: Urbanization, their problems and their remedies
Infrastructure: Urban
prelimsPoints: Ministry responsible for Urban Mobility Plan
Focus of the new plan (Tier 2/3 cities)
Key features (public transport, NMT, smart traffic)
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges of urban mobility in rapidly growing Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities in India.
Analyze the importance of an integrated approach to urban transportation for sustainable development.
Evaluate the role of government policies and private investment in promoting green mobility solutions.
Suggest innovative strategies to improve public transport and non-motorized transport infrastructure in smaller cities.
GS1.imageDescription: Modern public transport in a bustling city, representing urban development.
GS1.trivia: India is projected to have 50% of its population living in urban areas by 2047, making urban planning crucial.

GS1.headline: ASI Initiates Major Restoration Project for Ancient Heritage Sites
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) launched a large-scale restoration and conservation project for several ancient heritage sites across the country.
• The initiative focuses on preserving architectural integrity, using traditional building materials and techniques, and integrating modern conservation science.
• Sites include lesser-known temples, ancient stepwells, and historical monuments, aiming to boost cultural tourism and local economies.
• Community engagement and skill development for local artisans in heritage conservation are integral parts of the project.
• The project also involves digital documentation and 3D mapping of the sites for future reference and virtual accessibility.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of preserving India's rich cultural heritage for national identity and future generations.
Analyze the challenges faced by organizations like ASI in conserving ancient monuments amidst urbanization and environmental degradation.
Evaluate the potential of cultural tourism as a driver of economic growth and local development.
Suggest innovative approaches for community involvement and technological integration in heritage conservation efforts.
GS1.imageDescription: Workers carefully restoring an ancient temple facade.
GS1.trivia: The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) was founded in 1861 by Alexander Cunningham, who became its first Director-General.

GS2.headline: Government Unveils 'Viksit Bharat 2047' Advanced Manufacturing & Skill Development Policy
GS2.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved a comprehensive policy framework aimed at boosting advanced manufacturing capabilities and skill development across key sectors.
• The policy integrates with the 'PM Gati Shakti' master plan, ensuring seamless infrastructure support for new industrial corridors and clusters.
• Focus areas include robotics, AI-driven manufacturing, green technologies, and high-precision engineering, with significant incentives for R&D.
• A new National Skill Development Mission 2.0 is launched to re-skill and up-skill the workforce for future industries, linking education with industry demands.
• The initiative seeks to position India as a global manufacturing hub and achieve the 'Viksit Bharat' vision by 2047.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Development processes and the development industry
Skill development
mainsPoints: Significance of manufacturing for economic growth and employment generation.
Role of skill development in demographic dividend utilization.
Challenges and opportunities in transitioning to advanced manufacturing.
Impact of integrated infrastructure planning (PM Gati Shakti) on industrial development.
Government's long-term vision for India's economic future.
GS2.imageDescription: Illustration of modern factories and skilled workers, with 'Viksit Bharat 2047' logo.
GS2.trivia: The concept of 'Viksit Bharat' or Developed India by 2047 aligns with India's 100th year of independence.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Directs Government to Strengthen Data Privacy Framework
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark ruling, emphasized the urgent need for a robust data protection law and privacy framework in India.
• The court highlighted gaps in existing regulations, particularly concerning personal data collection by private entities and state surveillance.
• It directed the Union Government to expedite the legislative process for a comprehensive data protection bill, ensuring fundamental rights are upheld.
• The ruling stemmed from petitions challenging certain aspects of digital data collection and sharing practices.
• Experts believe this judgment will significantly influence the upcoming parliamentary session and the final shape of India's data privacy legislation.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions
Fundamental Rights
Information Technology
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court's stance on data privacy
Need for robust data protection law
mainsPoints: Discuss the implications of the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right in the digital age.
Analyze the challenges in balancing state security concerns with individual data privacy.
Evaluate the potential impact of a comprehensive data protection law on India's digital economy.
Critically examine the role of judiciary in safeguarding fundamental rights in the absence of specific legislation.
GS2.imageDescription: Gavel and digital data flowing, symbolizing justice and technology.
GS2.trivia: The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared the Right to Privacy a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution.

GS2.headline: India-ASEAN Summit Concludes with Focus on Enhanced Connectivity and Digital Partnership
GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-ASEAN Summit concluded with leaders reaffirming commitment to strengthening the Comprehensive Strategic Partnership.
• Key outcomes included agreements on boosting physical and digital connectivity, focusing on projects under the Act East Policy.
• Discussions emphasized cooperation in emerging technologies, cybersecurity, and digital public infrastructure development.
• Both sides stressed the importance of a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific region, adhering to international law.
• India reiterated its support for ASEAN centrality and its role in regional peace and prosperity.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India's interests
prelimsPoints: Key focus areas of India-ASEAN cooperation (connectivity, digital, Indo-Pacific)
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of ASEAN for India's Act East Policy and its broader Indo-Pacific strategy.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in enhancing connectivity between India and Southeast Asian nations.
Evaluate India's role in promoting a free and open Indo-Pacific region in collaboration with ASEAN.
Examine the potential of digital partnership in strengthening India-ASEAN ties.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and ASEAN member states.
GS2.trivia: India became a Sectoral Partner of ASEAN in 1992, a Dialogue Partner in 1995, and a Summit-level Partner in 2002.

GS2.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Expands Services, Focus on Telemedicine Integration
GS2.explainer:
• The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) announced the expansion of its services, aiming for wider adoption of digital health records and telemedicine.
• New features include enhanced interoperability between healthcare providers and integration with more private health applications.
• A major push is being made for telemedicine services, especially in rural and remote areas, to improve access to specialist consultations.
• The mission seeks to create a seamless online platform for patients to access their health information and medical services.
• Training programs for healthcare professionals on digital tools and data security are also being scaled up.
gsSubTopics: Development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
e-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential
prelimsPoints: Benefits for patients and healthcare providers
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of digital health initiatives like ABDM in improving healthcare access and efficiency in India.
Analyze the challenges in implementing a nationwide digital health ecosystem, particularly in terms of data privacy and infrastructure.
Evaluate the role of telemedicine in bridging the healthcare gap in rural and remote areas.
Suggest measures to ensure equitable access to digital health services for all sections of society.
GS2.imageDescription: Digital interface showing health records and telemedicine consultation.
GS2.trivia: The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission aims to develop the backbone necessary to support the integrated digital health infrastructure of the country.

GS2.headline: India Continues G20 Follow-up, Champions Global South Priorities
GS2.explainer:
• A year after its successful G20 presidency, India is actively engaged in follow-up initiatives, pushing for the implementation of outcomes from the New Delhi Declaration.
• India continues to champion the priorities of the Global South, advocating for equitable representation in global governance and addressing development challenges.
• Focus areas include climate finance, sustainable development goals (SDGs), and reforms of multilateral development banks.
• Bilateral and multilateral engagements are underway to build consensus on key global issues, including food and energy security.
• India's diplomatic efforts are seen as crucial in maintaining momentum for a more inclusive and representative global order.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India's interests
prelimsPoints: Key outcomes of India's G20 Presidency
mainsPoints: Analyze India's increasing role in shaping the global agenda through forums like G20, particularly as a voice for the Global South.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in reforming multilateral development banks to address contemporary global financial needs.
Evaluate India's efforts in mobilizing global action on climate finance and achieving Sustainable Development Goals.
Examine the significance of the New Delhi Declaration and its implications for future global cooperation.
GS2.imageDescription: G20 logo with flags of member countries, emphasizing India's leadership.
GS2.trivia: India's G20 theme 'Vasudhaiva Kutumbakam' or 'One Earth · One Family · One Future' resonated globally.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate, Focuses on Liquidity Management Amidst Global Volatility
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) concluded its latest Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) meeting, deciding to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
• The decision comes amidst persistent global economic uncertainties, geopolitical tensions, and moderate domestic inflation pressures.
• RBI Governor highlighted the central bank's commitment to maintaining financial stability and supporting growth while managing inflation expectations.
• The MPC reiterated its withdrawal of accommodation stance, signaling a cautious approach to future policy adjustments.
• Liquidity management measures are expected to be fine-tuned to ensure adequate credit flow to productive sectors of the economy.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning
mobilization of resources
growth
development and employment
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: Factors influencing MPC decision (global volatility, domestic inflation)
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by RBI in balancing inflation control and economic growth.
Discuss the effectiveness of monetary policy tools in addressing supply-side inflation.
Examine the impact of global economic developments on India's monetary policy decisions.
Evaluate the role of liquidity management in ensuring financial stability and credit availability.
GS3.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building with currency symbols.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in 2016 to bring transparency and accountability in deciding monetary policy.

GS3.headline: ISRO Intensifies Gaganyaan Preparations: Focus on Crew Module & Abort Tests
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced accelerated preparations for the Gaganyaan mission, India's first human spaceflight program.
• Key focus is on rigorous testing of the crew module's life support systems and re-entry capabilities.
• A series of uncrewed test flights, including critical abort missions to ensure crew safety, are scheduled in the coming months.
• Astronaut training is progressing well, with specialized modules focusing on mission specifics and emergency procedures.
• Gaganyaan aims to demonstrate India's capability to launch humans into low Earth orbit and bring them back safely.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology
indigenization of technology and developing new technology
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of human spaceflight missions like Gaganyaan for India's scientific and technological advancement.
Analyze the challenges involved in developing indigenous capabilities for human spaceflight.
Evaluate the potential spin-off benefits of space technology for various sectors in India.
Examine the role of international collaboration in India's space program.
GS3.imageDescription: Artist's impression of the Gaganyaan crew module in orbit.
GS3.trivia: The Gaganyaan mission aims to send a 3-member crew to an orbit of 400 km for a 3-day mission and bring them back safely.

GS3.headline: India Launches National Green Hydrogen Mission Phase II, Targets Export Hub Status
GS3.explainer:
• Building on the success of Phase I, the government launched Phase II of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, with enhanced targets for production and export.
• The new phase includes greater incentives for green hydrogen production, storage, and infrastructure development, attracting both domestic and international investments.
• Focus areas include developing green hydrogen corridors, promoting its use in hard-to-abate sectors like steel and fertilizers, and establishing R&D centers.
• The policy aims to reduce India's reliance on fossil fuel imports and position the country as a leading global exporter of green hydrogen and its derivatives.
• Environmental and economic benefits, including job creation, are central to the mission's long-term objectives.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation
environmental impact assessment
Infrastructure: Energy
prelimsPoints: Objectives of National Green Hydrogen Mission Phase II
Target sectors for green hydrogen use
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and climate change commitments.
Analyze the economic and technological challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production in India.
Evaluate the potential of green hydrogen in decarbonizing hard-to-abate industrial sectors.
Examine how government policies and incentives can foster a robust green hydrogen ecosystem.
GS3.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen production facility with wind turbines and solar panels.
GS3.trivia: Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water into hydrogen and oxygen using renewable electricity, making it a zero-emission fuel.

GS3.headline: Government Strengthens Cybersecurity Framework for Critical National Infrastructure
GS3.explainer:
• In response to increasing cyber threats, the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) announced a strengthened cybersecurity framework.
• The new guidelines mandate enhanced security protocols and regular audits for all critical national infrastructure sectors, including energy, finance, and telecommunications.
• A dedicated Cyber Incident Response Team (CIRT) for critical infrastructure is being established, with closer coordination among various agencies.
• The policy emphasizes public-private partnerships for threat intelligence sharing and capacity building.
• The move aims to protect vital services from sophisticated cyberattacks and ensure national security.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks
role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges
basics of cyber security
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving nature of cyber threats and their implications for national security and economic stability.
Analyze the challenges in securing critical national infrastructure from sophisticated cyberattacks.
Examine the role of government, private sector, and international cooperation in building a resilient cybersecurity ecosystem.
Suggest policy measures to enhance India's cyber defense capabilities and capacity building.
GS3.imageDescription: Digital lock icon over a cityscape, representing cybersecurity for infrastructure.
GS3.trivia: India's National Cyber Security Policy was launched in 2013 to protect information infrastructure and reduce cybercrime.

GS3.headline: GST Council Meets, Discusses Rate Rationalization and Compliance Simplification
GS3.explainer:
• The GST Council convened its quarterly meeting to review tax collection trends and discuss potential reforms to the Goods and Services Tax regime.
• A key agenda item was the rationalization of GST rates for certain goods and services, aimed at simplifying the tax structure and boosting consumption.
• Discussions also focused on enhancing compliance through technological improvements and addressing issues faced by small and medium enterprises (SMEs).
• States presented their revenue concerns and sought clarity on compensation mechanisms for any revenue shortfalls.
• The Council is expected to announce specific recommendations aimed at making the GST system more efficient and equitable.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning
mobilization of resources
growth
development and employment
Government Budgeting
prelimsPoints: Issues faced by states regarding GST
mainsPoints: Analyze the impact of GST on India's economy and fiscal federalism since its implementation.
Discuss the challenges and benefits of GST rate rationalization for businesses and consumers.
Examine the role of technology in improving GST compliance and preventing tax evasion.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the GST Council as a unique institution for cooperative federalism in India.
GS3.imageDescription: GST logo with Indian currency notes.
GS3.trivia: The GST Council is a joint forum of the Centre and States, chaired by the Union Finance Minister, making it a unique example of cooperative federalism.

GS3.headline: PM-KISAN: Nationwide Verification Drive to Ensure Eligibility and Prevent Fraud
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare initiated a nationwide verification drive for beneficiaries under the Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN) scheme.
• The drive aims to identify and remove ineligible beneficiaries while ensuring that financial assistance reaches genuine farmers.
• Leveraging Aadhaar and land record integration, the verification process seeks to enhance transparency and reduce fraudulent claims.
• Farmers are urged to update their KYC details and land records to avoid disruption in benefits.
• The government reaffirms its commitment to supporting small and marginal farmers through direct income support.
gsSubTopics: Major crops-cropping patterns in various parts of the country
different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage
transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints
e-technology in the aid of farmers
prelimsPoints: Role of Aadhaar and land records
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of direct income support schemes like PM-KISAN for enhancing farmers' income and welfare.
Analyze the challenges associated with beneficiary identification and prevention of fraud in large-scale welfare schemes.
Evaluate the role of technology (Aadhaar, land records) in improving the efficiency and transparency of DBT programs.
Suggest measures to strengthen the implementation and monitoring of agricultural support schemes.
GS3.imageDescription: Farmer in a field, with a digital overlay showing DBT in action.
GS3.trivia: PM-KISAN scheme provides an income support of Rs. 6,000 per year to all eligible farmer families across the country.

GS3.headline: Defence Ministry Boosts Indigenous Manufacturing with New Procurement Policy
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence introduced a revised Defence Acquisition Procedure (DAP) aimed at further promoting indigenous manufacturing and reducing import dependence.
• The new policy prioritizes procurement from Indian vendors, with specific provisions for start-ups and MSMEs in the defence sector.
• Emphasis is placed on 'Design and Development' within India, fostering innovation and self-reliance in critical defence technologies.
• Long-term agreements for technology transfer and co-production with foreign partners are encouraged, but with mandatory indigenous content requirements.
• The move aligns with the 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiative, seeking to transform India into a defence manufacturing hub.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Organized crime and terrorism
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
indigenization of technology and developing new technology
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic necessity of indigenous defence manufacturing for India's national security and economic sovereignty.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in achieving self-reliance in critical defence technologies.
Evaluate the impact of government policies like 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' and revised DAP on the growth of India's defence industrial base.
Suggest measures to foster innovation and private sector participation in the defence manufacturing ecosystem.
GS3.imageDescription: Modern defence equipment manufactured in India.
GS3.trivia: India is one of the largest importers of defence equipment globally, but aims to become an exporter through indigenization efforts.

GS3.headline: ICAR Promotes Climate-Resilient Agriculture Practices Across States
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR) intensified its efforts to promote climate-resilient agriculture (CRA) practices among farmers nationwide.
• Focus areas include drought-resistant crop varieties, efficient water management techniques, and agroforestry systems.
• ICAR is collaborating with state agricultural universities and Krishi Vigyan Kendras (KVKs) to disseminate knowledge and provide training to farmers.
• The initiative aims to enhance farmers' adaptive capacity to climate change impacts and ensure food security in vulnerable regions.
• Policy support for adoption of CRA practices, including subsidies and market linkages, is also being advocated.
gsSubTopics: Major crops-cropping patterns in various parts of the country
different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage
transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints
e-technology in the aid of farmers
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation
environmental impact assessment
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing vulnerability of Indian agriculture to climate change and its implications for food security.
Analyze the various strategies and practices involved in promoting climate-resilient agriculture.
Evaluate the role of research institutions and extension services (like KVKs) in disseminating agricultural innovations to farmers.
Suggest policy interventions to encourage widespread adoption of sustainable and climate-resilient farming practices.
GS3.imageDescription: Farmers inspecting drought-resistant crops in a field.
GS3.trivia: ICAR is an autonomous organization under the Department of Agricultural Research and Education (DARE), Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare, Government of India.

GS3.headline: India Semiconductor Mission Reports Significant Progress, Attracts New Fab Investments
GS3.explainer:
• The India Semiconductor Mission (ISM) announced significant progress in its efforts to establish a robust semiconductor manufacturing ecosystem in the country.
• Several new proposals for semiconductor fabrication (fab) units and ATMP (Assembly, Testing, Marking, and Packaging) facilities have been approved, attracting both domestic and foreign investors.
• The mission is focusing on developing a skilled workforce for the semiconductor industry through specialized training programs and academic collaborations.
• Incentives under the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme are proving effective in stimulating investment in this high-tech sector.
• India aims to become a key player in the global semiconductor supply chain, reducing reliance on imports and enhancing strategic autonomy.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Investment models
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of building an indigenous semiconductor manufacturing ecosystem for India's economic and technological sovereignty.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in attracting significant investments and developing a skilled workforce for the semiconductor industry.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government policies, such as the PLI scheme and ISM, in fostering growth in high-tech manufacturing.
Examine the geopolitical implications of global semiconductor supply chain vulnerabilities and India's role in addressing them.
GS3.imageDescription: Microchip on a circuit board, symbolizing semiconductor manufacturing.
GS3.trivia: The global semiconductor industry is highly concentrated, with Taiwan and South Korea dominating advanced chip manufacturing.

GS3.headline: Animal Welfare Board Proposes Stricter Rules for Pet Shops and Breeders
GS3.explainer:
• The Animal Welfare Board of India (AWBI) submitted a proposal to the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change for stricter regulations on pet shops and animal breeders.
• The proposed rules aim to curb illegal breeding, prevent cruelty, and ensure humane treatment of animals sold as pets.
• Key provisions include mandatory registration for all breeders and pet shops, strict health and hygiene standards, and limits on animal stocking.
• Public consultations were held, with strong support from animal rights organizations for enhanced protection of companion animals.
• The new regulations are expected to bring much-needed accountability and transparency to the pet trade industry.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation
environmental impact assessment
keyTerms: Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act
prelimsPoints: Aim of the rules (curb illegal breeding, humane treatment)
mainsPoints: Discuss the ethical and legal aspects of animal welfare in India, particularly concerning companion animals.
Analyze the challenges in regulating the unorganized pet trade and breeding industry.
Evaluate the effectiveness of existing laws and propose measures for stronger enforcement of animal protection norms.
Examine the societal implications of animal cruelty and the importance of responsible pet ownership.
GS3.imageDescription: Healthy puppies in a clean environment, symbolizing good animal welfare.
GS3.trivia: The Animal Welfare Board of India is a statutory advisory body established under Section 4 of the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act, 1960.

GS3.headline: Indian Railways Announces New Dedicated Freight Corridor for North-East Region
GS3.explainer:
• Indian Railways unveiled plans for a new dedicated freight corridor aimed at enhancing connectivity and economic development in the North-East region.
• The corridor will facilitate faster movement of goods, reduce logistics costs, and provide better access to markets for agricultural and industrial products.
• The project is part of the broader 'Gati Shakti' initiative, integrating with multi-modal transport infrastructure.
• Environmental impact assessments and land acquisition processes are underway, with a commitment to sustainable development.
• This strategic infrastructure push is expected to boost trade with neighboring countries and unlock the region's economic potential.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Development processes and the development industry
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of improving railway infrastructure in India's North-East region for national integration and economic development.
Analyze how dedicated freight corridors can enhance logistics efficiency and reduce transportation costs for industries and agriculture.
Evaluate the role of multi-modal connectivity projects like PM Gati Shakti in transforming India's infrastructure landscape.
Examine the potential impact of improved connectivity on the 'Act East Policy' and India's trade with Southeast Asian countries.
GS3.imageDescription: A modern freight train speeding through a scenic landscape in North East India.
GS3.trivia: The North-East Frontier Railway (NFR) is one of the 17 railway zones in India and covers the entire North-Eastern region.
